rag_rules: Place input range assumptions in component type annexes using visible feature names and concrete bounds.
Use valid AGREE syntax for assumption declarations with proper type alignment.
Generate syntactically valid AGREE annex blocks with correct scoping for component types and implementations.
generic_rules: Assumptions and guarantees should reference visible identifiers from the AADL model.
Use declared variables or features in AGREE contracts to ensure scope validity.
Avoid encoding AADL properties as AGREE expressions unless explicitly typed in scope.
Ensure type consistency between helper declarations and expressions in AGREE contracts.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees are declared at the component type level unless binding to implementation features.
Boolean expressions in AGREE must match the actual data type of the referenced features.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level assignments in component type contracts.
Do not reference AADL property names directly as AGREE variables.
Do not generate unsafe range comparisons over opaque Boolean or enumeration data types.
irrelevant_topics: Model fusion and owner scope rules
